user_prompt: Can you summarize Article 28 in 3 bullet points?
actual_output:
# Article 28 (Data Processing Agreements) — Summary

• **Processor Obligation:** Any natural or legal person processing personal data on behalf of a controller must act only on documented instructions and implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures (Art. 28(1), 28(3)).

• **Written Contract Required:** A contract or other legally binding instrument must govern the processor relationship, specifying confidentiality, security, sub-processor authorization, and data subject rights assistance (Art. 28(3)).

• **Processor Liability:** Processors are liable for damages if they fail to fulfill GDPR obligations, and controllers remain liable for processor conduct unless the processor proves it was not responsible for the damage (Art. 28(4)).

---

**Practical implication:** If you use any third party (cloud provider, email service, analytics tool, payment processor) that touches EU user data, you need a signed Data Processing Agreement in place. Missing DPAs are among the most common audit findings and carry significant fine risk.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly and accurately summarized Article 28 in three bullet points as requested, adhering to its role as a GDPR Auditor.
